The Utah Retiree's 2026 Financial Calendar: 12 Months to Retirement Success

A comprehensive month-by-month financial planning guide specifically designed for Utah retirees to optimize retirement security and leverage state advantages.

Mike Stevens Capital Wealth Advisors
17 MIN READ 1/16/2026
retirement planning tax strategies financial planning utah advantages

Originally aired on KAOX, KID, KNRS, and KSL

The Utah Retiree's 2026 Financial Calendar: 12 Months to Retirement Success

Published: January 17, 2026
Last Updated: March 18, 2026
Author: Mike Stevens, Capital Wealth Advisors
Episode: Retire Right Radio, January 17, 2026

Originally aired on KAOX, KID, KNRS, and KSL. This comprehensive guide is based on the January 17, 2026 episode of Retire Right Radio with Mike Stevens, founder and president of Capital Wealth Advisors.

Introduction: Your Year-Long Roadmap to Retirement Confidence

Planning for retirement isn't just about having enough money—it's about having the right strategies at the right times throughout the year. For Utah retirees and pre-retirees, 2026 presents unique opportunities to optimize your financial position, reduce tax burdens, and secure your retirement dreams.

Mike Stevens of Capital Wealth Advisors has created a month-by-month financial planning calendar specifically designed for Utah residents. This isn't generic national advice—it's tailored to Utah's tax advantages, cost structure, and unique lifestyle opportunities.

Whether you're five years from retirement or already enjoying your golden years, this calendar provides actionable steps you can take each month to maximize your retirement security and make the most of Utah's advantages.

Key Takeaways: Your 2026 Financial Planning Priorities

  • Tax Planning: Strategic timing of Roth conversions, charitable distributions, and asset sales throughout the year
  • Utah Advantages: No Social Security tax, moderate income tax rates, and lower cost of living create unique opportunities
  • Healthcare Timing: Medicare enrollment periods, supplement reviews, and long-term care planning deadlines
  • Estate Planning: Annual gifting limits, trust funding opportunities, and document reviews
  • Investment Strategy: Quarterly rebalancing, tax-loss harvesting, and systematic withdrawal planning

January: New Year, New Financial Foundation

Priority Actions for Utah Retirees

Tax Document Organization

  • Gather all 2025 tax documents (1099s, 1098s, K-1s)
  • Review Utah state tax requirements and advantages
  • Calculate estimated Social Security tax savings (Utah doesn't tax benefits)
  • Plan Roth conversion opportunities for the year

Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) Planning

  • Calculate 2026 RMD requirements for IRA and 401(k) accounts
  • Consider timing distributions to manage tax brackets
  • Explore Qualified Charitable Distributions (QCDs) to Utah charities
  • Review beneficiary designations on all retirement accounts

Insurance Review

  • Evaluate Medicare Supplement insurance options
  • Review long-term care insurance policies
  • Assess life insurance needs for estate planning
  • Verify coverage with Utah healthcare providers

Utah-Specific January Opportunities

Municipal Bond Review: Research Utah municipal bonds for tax-free income opportunities Healthcare Provider Network: Confirm all doctors and specialists remain in your Medicare network Property Tax Planning: Review property tax assessments and appeal deadlines if necessary

Real Utah Client Example: The Hendersons from Murray

The Hendersons, ages 68 and 65, used January to reorganize their entire tax strategy. By planning strategic Roth conversions throughout 2026, they projected saving $34,000 in lifetime taxes while maintaining their desired income level.

February: Love Your Retirement Plan (and Review It)

Financial Health Check-Up

Investment Portfolio Review

  • Analyze 2025 performance against benchmarks
  • Assess asset allocation alignment with retirement timeline
  • Review fee structures and investment costs
  • Consider rebalancing needs after market movements

Income Planning Assessment

  • Project 2026 income from all sources (Social Security, pensions, investments)
  • Calculate withdrawal rates from retirement accounts
  • Plan for any large expenses (travel, home improvements, family events)
  • Budget for Utah-specific costs (recreation, seasonal activities)

Estate Planning Updates

  • Review and update wills, trusts, and beneficiary designations
  • Consider annual gifting strategies to reduce estate taxes
  • Evaluate charitable giving plans for 2026
  • Discuss legacy goals with family members

Utah Advantage: February Tax Planning

State Tax Benefits: Utah's favorable tax treatment of retirement income creates opportunities for strategic planning Charitable Deductions: Plan giving to Utah organizations for maximum tax benefit Healthcare Deductions: Budget for medical expenses with Utah's excellent healthcare systems

Case Study: Single Retiree from Salt Lake City

Patricia, age 71, used February to restructure her charitable giving. By switching from cash donations to Qualified Charitable Distributions from her IRA, she satisfied her RMD requirements while supporting local Utah charities tax-free, saving $4,200 annually.

March: Spring Cleaning for Your Finances

Quarterly Review and Adjustments

Investment Rebalancing

  • Assess first quarter market performance
  • Rebalance portfolios back to target allocations
  • Consider tax-loss harvesting opportunities
  • Review dividend reinvestment strategies

Healthcare Planning

  • Schedule annual medical checkups and screenings
  • Review Medicare coverage and utilization
  • Plan for any elective procedures or treatments
  • Update healthcare directives and medical power of attorney

Tax Preparation Finalization

  • Complete 2025 tax returns with Utah-specific deductions
  • Calculate actual tax savings from Utah's no Social Security tax policy
  • Plan estimated tax payments for 2026
  • Review tax withholding on retirement distributions

Utah Recreation and Lifestyle Budgeting

Outdoor Activity Planning: Budget for Utah's world-class recreation opportunities Travel Coordination: Plan visits to Utah's five National Parks during optimal seasons Family Events: Budget for graduations, weddings, and family gatherings

April: Tax Day and Beyond

Post-Tax Season Planning

IRA Contribution Maximization

  • Make final 2025 IRA contributions before deadline
  • Plan 2026 contribution strategies
  • Consider spousal IRA opportunities
  • Evaluate Roth vs. traditional IRA benefits

Estate Tax Considerations

  • Utilize annual gift tax exclusions before year-end
  • Consider generation-skipping transfer opportunities
  • Review trust funding needs and opportunities
  • Plan for potential estate tax law changes

Insurance Optimization

  • Review life insurance cash values and policy performance
  • Consider long-term care insurance needs and options
  • Evaluate disability insurance for working spouses
  • Assess umbrella insurance coverage adequacy

Utah-Specific April Strategies

State Tax Planning: Finalize strategies for minimizing Utah state income tax Municipal Investments: Consider Utah municipal bond ladders for tax-free income Healthcare Coordination: Ensure smooth coordination between Medicare and Utah providers

May: Spring Into Action

Investment Strategy Refinement

Second Quarter Assessment

  • Review portfolio performance through market volatility
  • Assess international diversification needs
  • Consider alternative investment opportunities
  • Evaluate dividend income sustainability

Retirement Income Optimization

  • Fine-tune withdrawal strategies from retirement accounts
  • Optimize Social Security timing for maximum benefit
  • Coordinate pension distributions with tax planning
  • Plan for summer recreational expenses and travel

Long-Term Care Planning

  • Evaluate long-term care insurance needs
  • Research Utah care facilities and home health options
  • Discuss care preferences with family members
  • Plan for aging in place vs. facility care decisions

Utah Lifestyle Integration

Recreation Planning: Budget for Utah's peak outdoor seasons Healthcare Maintenance: Schedule preventive care during optimal weather Family Coordination: Plan multi-generational activities and travel

June: Mid-Year Financial Physical

Comprehensive Plan Review

Six-Month Performance Analysis

  • Compare actual vs. projected income and expenses
  • Assess investment performance against benchmarks
  • Review tax withholding and estimated payment accuracy
  • Evaluate progress toward annual financial goals

Risk Management Assessment

  • Review insurance coverage adequacy
  • Assess emergency fund sufficiency
  • Evaluate market volatility impact on retirement security
  • Consider hedge strategies for major risks

Healthcare Cost Management

  • Review actual healthcare expenses vs. budget
  • Evaluate Medicare supplement effectiveness
  • Plan for potential long-term care needs
  • Consider Health Savings Account strategies if applicable

Utah Summer Planning

Recreation Budgeting: Plan for peak season activities and travel Property Maintenance: Budget for Utah home maintenance needs Family Visits: Plan for grandchildren visits and activities

Case Study: The Johnsons from Park City

Mid-year review revealed the Johnsons were ahead of their income projection by $12,000. Instead of increasing spending, they used this surplus for additional Roth conversions, saving an estimated $18,000 in future taxes.

July: Independence and Financial Freedom

Summer Financial Strategies

Tax Loss Harvesting

  • Identify underperforming investments for tax loss harvesting
  • Coordinate with other taxable events for maximum benefit
  • Consider wash sale rules and replacement investments
  • Plan for year-end capital gains distributions

Estate Planning Actions

  • Execute annual gifting strategies
  • Fund trusts and educational accounts
  • Review charitable giving progress for the year
  • Consider family limited partnership opportunities

Retirement Income Adjustments

  • Adjust withdrawal rates based on market performance
  • Optimize sequence of withdrawals from different account types
  • Plan for any large expenses in the second half of the year
  • Review Social Security optimization opportunities

Utah-Specific July Opportunities

Property Tax Appeals: File appeals for Utah property tax assessments if needed Municipal Bond Opportunities: Research new Utah municipal bond issues Healthcare Planning: Take advantage of Utah's excellent summer weather for medical procedures

August: Back-to-School Financial Education

Investment Education and Strategy

Portfolio Optimization

  • Review third quarter investment performance
  • Assess need for investment strategy adjustments
  • Consider new investment opportunities
  • Evaluate manager performance and fees

Retirement Distribution Planning

  • Plan fourth quarter retirement account withdrawals
  • Optimize tax bracket management through year-end
  • Consider accelerating or deferring income
  • Plan for Required Minimum Distributions

Healthcare Planning

  • Prepare for Medicare Annual Enrollment Period
  • Research Medicare Advantage and supplement options
  • Evaluate prescription drug coverage needs
  • Plan for any healthcare changes

Educational Planning for Grandchildren

529 Plan Contributions: Maximize educational savings for Utah grandchildren UTMA/UGMA Accounts: Consider custodial account strategies Educational Tax Credits: Plan for American Opportunity and Lifetime Learning Credits

September: Fall Into Good Financial Habits

Fourth Quarter Preparation

Tax Planning Intensification

  • Project year-end tax situation
  • Plan additional Roth conversions if beneficial
  • Consider accelerating or deferring deductions
  • Optimize charitable giving timing

Investment Portfolio Maintenance

  • Prepare for typical fall market volatility
  • Review international and domestic allocation
  • Consider rebalancing needs before year-end
  • Assess dividend payment schedules and tax implications

Insurance Annual Reviews

  • Evaluate long-term care insurance needs
  • Review life insurance beneficiaries and cash values
  • Assess auto and homeowner's insurance coverage
  • Consider umbrella policy adequacy

Utah Autumn Strategies

Healthcare Enrollment: Prepare for Medicare open enrollment period Property Management: Plan for Utah winter property maintenance Recreation Budget: Budget for fall activities and potential early ski season

October: Medicare and Healthcare Planning

Medicare Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 - December 7)

Coverage Evaluation

  • Compare Medicare Advantage plans available in Utah
  • Review Medicare supplement insurance options
  • Evaluate prescription drug plan coverage and costs
  • Consider health savings account coordination

Provider Network Review

  • Confirm preferred Utah doctors remain in network
  • Evaluate specialist access and referral requirements
  • Review hospital and facility coverage
  • Consider out-of-state coverage for travel

Cost-Benefit Analysis

  • Compare total annual costs including premiums, deductibles, and copays
  • Evaluate coverage for Utah-specific healthcare needs
  • Consider long-term care insurance integration
  • Plan for potential Medicare premium increases

Healthcare Budgeting for 2027

Utah Healthcare Advantages:

  • Intermountain Healthcare system excellence
  • University of Utah medical center access
  • Competitive healthcare costs compared to coastal areas
  • Strong home health care networks

Real Client Example: Medicare Optimization

Robert from Ogden saved $3,800 annually by switching from a Medicare Supplement plan to a Medicare Advantage plan that better covered his Utah healthcare providers while providing additional benefits like dental and vision coverage.

November: Thanksgiving for Tax Planning

Year-End Tax Optimization

Final Roth Conversion Opportunities

  • Calculate remaining tax bracket room for conversions
  • Execute conversions before December 31 deadline
  • Consider multi-year conversion strategies
  • Coordinate with other taxable events

Charitable Giving Maximization

  • Complete annual charitable giving goals
  • Consider donor-advised funds for tax efficiency
  • Execute Qualified Charitable Distributions from IRAs
  • Evaluate appreciated asset donations

Tax Loss Harvesting Completion

  • Realize losses to offset capital gains
  • Consider wash sale rule implications
  • Plan replacement investments
  • Coordinate with mutual fund capital gains distributions

Estate Planning Year-End Actions

Annual Gift Tax Exclusions

  • Complete gifts to family members before year-end
  • Fund 529 education savings accounts
  • Consider generation-skipping opportunities
  • Execute trust funding strategies

Utah-Specific November Strategies

Property Tax Planning: Complete any property tax appeals or adjustments Charitable Giving: Support Utah organizations before year-end for tax benefits Healthcare Coordination: Finalize Medicare changes for January 1 effective date

December: Year-End Financial Wrap-Up

Final 2026 Actions

Required Minimum Distribution Completion

  • Ensure all RMDs are taken before December 31
  • Consider additional distributions if beneficial for tax planning
  • Coordinate with investment rebalancing needs
  • Plan for 2027 RMD requirements

Investment Portfolio Finalization

  • Complete any needed rebalancing
  • Realize remaining tax losses
  • Plan for January rebalancing if needed
  • Review investment cost basis tracking

Estate Planning Completion

  • Complete annual gifting before December 31
  • Update beneficiary designations as needed
  • Review trust distributions and tax implications
  • Plan for 2027 estate planning strategies

2027 Planning Preparation

Budget Development: Create comprehensive 2027 retirement budget Investment Strategy: Plan investment allocation for new year Tax Planning: Project 2027 tax situation and planning opportunities Healthcare Planning: Confirm 2027 Medicare and healthcare coverage

Utah Winter Planning

Recreation Budgeting: Plan for Utah's world-class winter recreation Property Maintenance: Prepare Utah homes for winter weather Travel Planning: Consider winter travel to warmer climates

Special Utah Retirement Considerations Throughout 2026

Tax Advantages Unique to Utah

No Social Security Tax

  • Provides additional spending power throughout retirement
  • Creates opportunities for strategic income timing
  • Allows higher withdrawal rates compared to other states
  • Reduces overall tax burden on retirement income

Moderate Income Tax Rates

  • Competitive rates for retirement income
  • Favorable treatment of pension and retirement account distributions
  • Strategic Roth conversion opportunities
  • Municipal bond advantages for Utah residents

Healthcare Excellence

Intermountain Healthcare System

  • Consistently ranked among nation's best healthcare systems
  • Comprehensive network throughout Utah
  • Integrated care coordination
  • Competitive costs compared to national averages

University of Utah Health

  • Leading research and treatment capabilities
  • Specialty care excellence
  • Clinical trial opportunities
  • Teaching hospital advantages

Lifestyle and Recreation

Outdoor Recreation Paradise

  • Five National Parks within driving distance
  • World-class skiing at multiple resorts
  • Hiking, fishing, and camping opportunities
  • Four-season recreational activities

Cost of Living Advantages

  • Lower housing costs than coastal areas
  • Reasonable utility and transportation costs
  • Competitive dining and entertainment options
  • High quality of life at reasonable cost

Real Utah Success Stories: Year-Long Planning in Action

Case Study: The Williams Family - Retirement Transition

Background: Tom (64) and Susan (62) Williams from Sandy were preparing for Tom's retirement from the Utah Transit Authority while Susan continued her teaching career.

2026 Planning Calendar Implementation:

January-March: Established Roth conversion strategy to manage future tax brackets April-June: Optimized Tom's pension timing with Social Security claiming strategy July-September: Implemented tax-loss harvesting to offset Roth conversion taxes October-December: Selected optimal Medicare coverage for Tom's January retirement

Results:

  • Reduced lifetime tax burden by $89,000
  • Increased after-tax retirement income by 15%
  • Secured optimal healthcare coverage
  • Created systematic approach for ongoing planning

Case Study: Single Retiree - Advanced Planning

Background: Margaret Thompson, 72, from Salt Lake City, had substantial IRA assets and wanted to minimize taxes while supporting local charities.

Year-Long Strategy:

  • Monthly QCDs: $8,333 monthly distributions to Utah charities
  • Quarterly Reviews: Investment rebalancing and tax optimization
  • Annual Planning: Estate planning updates and family coordination
  • Healthcare Management: Medicare optimization and long-term care planning

Results:

  • Satisfied RMD requirements tax-free through charitable distributions
  • Supported favorite Utah organizations with $100,000 annually
  • Reduced taxable income and Medicare premium costs
  • Created systematic legacy planning approach

Your 2026 Financial Planning Calendar Action Steps

Monthly Priorities Summary

Every Month:

  • Review investment performance and market conditions
  • Monitor cash flow and expense management
  • Assess healthcare needs and costs
  • Update emergency fund as needed

Quarterly (March, June, September, December):

  • Comprehensive portfolio review and rebalancing
  • Tax planning analysis and strategy adjustments
  • Healthcare coverage evaluation
  • Estate planning document review

Annually:

  • Complete tax planning and filing
  • Medicare enrollment period evaluation
  • Insurance coverage comprehensive review
  • Estate planning updates and family discussions

Utah-Specific Monthly Actions

January: Tax document organization, RMD planning February: Investment portfolio review, estate planning updates
March: Quarterly rebalancing, healthcare checkups April: Tax filing completion, IRA contributions May: Summer recreation budgeting, long-term care planning June: Mid-year comprehensive review July: Tax loss harvesting, estate gifting August: Education planning for grandchildren September: Fourth quarter tax planning preparation October: Medicare Annual Enrollment Period November: Year-end tax optimization, charitable giving December: RMD completion, 2027 planning preparation

Get Professional Guidance for Your 2026 Financial Calendar

Capital Wealth Advisors' Year-Round Support

Quarterly Reviews: Comprehensive plan assessments every three months Tax Planning: Strategic coordination throughout the year Investment Management: Professional portfolio management and rebalancing Estate Planning: Coordination with estate attorneys and tax professionals Healthcare Planning: Medicare and long-term care strategy development

Special Offer for Utah Residents

Complimentary Retirement Money Map™: For the next five callers to 801-210-5500, Capital Wealth will provide a comprehensive 2026 planning analysis including:

  • Month-by-month planning calendar customized for your situation
  • Tax optimization strategies specific to Utah advantages
  • Investment allocation analysis and recommendations
  • Healthcare cost projections and coverage optimization
  • Estate planning coordination and legacy planning

Contact Information:

  • Phone: 801-210-5500
  • Text: "VISIT" to 801-210-5500
  • Website: capitalwealth.com

Conclusion: Your Path to 2026 Retirement Success

A successful retirement doesn't happen by accident—it happens by design. This 2026 Financial Planning Calendar provides the roadmap, but success requires implementation, monitoring, and professional guidance.

Utah retirees have unique advantages:

  • Favorable tax treatment of retirement income
  • Excellent healthcare systems and networks
  • Lower cost of living with high quality of life
  • Outstanding recreation and lifestyle opportunities
  • Strong family and community support systems

Your 2026 success depends on:

  1. Systematic approach: Following a month-by-month planning process
  2. Utah optimization: Leveraging state-specific advantages and opportunities
  3. Professional guidance: Working with fiduciary advisors who understand Utah
  4. Regular monitoring: Quarterly reviews and adjustments as needed
  5. Long-term focus: Keeping retirement security and legacy goals in mind

Don't leave your 2026 retirement success to chance. Use this calendar as your guide, seek professional assistance when needed, and take advantage of every opportunity Utah provides for retirement security.

Your future self will thank you for the planning you do today.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How often should I review my retirement plan during 2026?

A: Monthly monitoring of cash flow and expenses, quarterly comprehensive reviews, and annual major planning updates provide optimal oversight without over-management.

Q: What makes Utah retirement planning different from other states?

A: Utah's no Social Security tax, moderate income tax rates, excellent healthcare systems, and lower cost of living create unique optimization opportunities not available in other states.

Q: Should I handle this planning myself or seek professional help?

A: While this calendar provides excellent guidance, retirement planning involves complex tax, investment, and estate planning decisions that benefit from professional fiduciary advice.

Q: What's the most important month for retirement planning?

A: Every month has important elements, but October (Medicare enrollment), November (year-end tax planning), and December (RMD completion) are particularly critical for avoiding costly mistakes.

Q: How do I coordinate all these different planning elements?

A: Professional advisors like Capital Wealth Advisors specialize in coordinating all aspects of retirement planning to ensure optimal outcomes and avoid conflicting strategies.
